{{Quote|With the power of the Force at his command, Anakin must stop Count Dooku from creating a new, more destructive droid army!|Game abstract|<ref name="Star Wars: The New Droid Army">''[[Star Wars: The New Droid Army]]''</ref>}}
'''''Star Wars: The New Droid Army''''' is a [[Star Wars Legends|Legends]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] set after the events of the film [[Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones|''Star Wars'': Episode II ''Attack of the Clones'']]. It was developed by [[Helixe]] and published by [[THQ]] on [[November 14]], [[2002]].

==Publisher's summary==
''Anakin is called before Mace Windu and Yoda in the Jedi temple and learns that an informant named Raala on Tatooine has sent word of a new type of battle droid on the planet. Contact with her has since been lost. Anakin is tasked to travel to Tatooine to investigate these new droids and discover the fate of their informant.''

==Opening crawl==
{{OpeningCrawl
|name=THE NEW DROID ARMY
|text=The Clone Wars are raging
throughout the galaxy. Under
increasing pressure from the
Confederacy of Independent
Systems, thousands more
systems have joined the
Separatist movement. Weary of
unending conflict, a climate of
foreboding has descended upon
the Republic.

In a valiant attempt to restore
peace the Jedi Council has sent
Jedi Knights into battle against
the Separatists. But Master Yoda
senses that the Council's power
is fading, and with it, their hope
of defeating the evil Count
Dooku.

Now, a desperate message
from Tatooine brings word of a
new, more sinister threat.
Master Yoda has sent the young
Jedi, ANAKIN SKYWALKER, to
investigate....}}

==Plot summary==
===Tatooine===
{{Main|Mission to Tatooine (Anakin Skywalker)}}
Shortly after the outbreak of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], the [[Jedi Council/Legends|Jedi Council]] receives a distress call from Tatooine and sends [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] to investigate. He is assaulted by groups of [[Womp rat/Legends|Womp rats]] and [[B1-series battle droid/Legends|battle droids]] and is informed in [[Mos Espa/Legends|Mos Espa]] that something must be disturbing the Womp rats' nests. He travels through [[Xelric Draw/Legends|Xelric Draw]] to the Womp rat caves and kills the [[Anooba/Legends|Anoobas]] that were apparently responsible for the disturbance. As he leaves the caves, Skywalker encounters [[Aurra Sing/Legends|Aurra Sing]], who attempts to collect a bounty posted on his head and it was her who sent him to the [[Womp rat cave]] hoping he would be lost there. She leaves throwing a [[Thermal detonator/Legends|thermal detonator]] and sending several of her "associates" after him with [[Vibro-ax/Legends|vibro-axes]] before fleeing.

Arriving back in town, a man informs him that [[Watto/Legends|Watto]] could probably help him find [[Raala Ponchar]], the sender of the transmission. However, assassins hired by the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutts]] are looking for Anakin. After fighting his way through, Anakin finds Watto, who informs him that Ponchar is his customer, buying evaporator equipment from him, and she can be found at a [[Moisture farm/Legends|moisture farm]] north of town. Before Anakin leaves, Watto invites him to return working for him should the Jedi beusiness become too difficult. On his way to the farm, another moisture farmer tells Anakin that [[Tusken Raider/Legends|Tusken Raiders]] have kidnapped some other farmers, and Anakin agrees to free them, having "no love for the Tusken Raiders." After this, one of the farmers leads him to [[Bellek]]'s homestead, where Ponchar is, but they come across a destroyed homestead on the way. The man says that the droids are like none he has ever seen, led by a "witch," had wrecked the place, and now the sand people are looking for salvageable materials. A few homesteads later, Anakin finds Bellek, who says that Ponchar was kidnapped by [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba the Hutt]]. Bellek warns Anakin that he will have to travel through the [[Jundland Wastes/Legends|Jundland Wastes]], which are "prime Tusken Raider territory." Anakin assures Bellek that he "know[s] how to deal with Tusken Raiders" and asks about the "witch," but Bellek decides he should get back to work on his [[Moisture farm/Legends|moisture farm]].

[[File:Saato_vs_Anakin.jpg|thumb|left|Anakin Skywalker duels Saato.]]
Anakin arrives at [[Jabba's Palace/Legends|Jabba's Palace]] and fights his way through guards and battle droids only to meet Aurra Sing at the front door of the palace, who claims she will enjoy watching Skywalker die. Just then, an unknown figure appears dressed in red and wielding two red lightsabers, telling Sing to save her strength, and [[Telekinesis/Legends|Force chokes]] Anakin. The new arrival then tells Sing to store Anakin in the dungeons, and that she will inform her master "that the Jedi is no longer a threat to our operation." Anakin awakes in the dungeons of Jabba's palace and there finds Raala Ponchar. He easily opens the door to their cell with [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] and the two escape together. Ponchar informs Anakin that Jabba is being paid by [[Wat Tambor/Legends|Wat Tambor]] to assist in smuggling construction materials and [[Cortosis/Legends|cortosis]] to [[Metalorn/Legends|Metalorn]]. The two free the other prisoners from the dungeon and Anakin escapes on a [[Skiff/Legends|skiff]], which is shot down in the desert by another skiff. The [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] who captured him previously, named [[Saato]], confronts him near a [[Sarlacc/Legends|Sarlacc]] pit, but she is defeated. Anakin contacts his [[Jedi Master/Legends|master]], [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]], and tells him what has happened. Obi-Wan, however, tells Anakin to return to [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], as it is [[Coruscant Insurrection|under attack]].

===Coruscant===
Anakin arrives at [[Galactic City/Legends|Galactic City]] and finds Obi-Wan waiting for him there. Obi-Wan explains that [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Separatist]] [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droids]] are attacking the underlevels of Coruscant and instructs Anakin to help fight off the invasion, and then leaves for the [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]] to gather reinforcements. Anakin feels a [[Disturbance in the Force/Legends|disturbance in the Force]] and encounters a [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]]-wielding man named [[Trenox]], who accuses Anakin of having caused him some trouble, then flees. Anakin pursues him to the underlevels. There, he meets fellow [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] [[Barriss Offee/Legends|Barriss Offee]], whose troops have been overrun by [[C-B3 cortosis battle droid/Legends|C-B3 cortosis battle droids]]. Anakin says he'll take care of it and assists [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]] in eliminating B1 and C-B3 battle droids as well as [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] combatants, [[ASN-121/Legends|ASN-121]] [[Assassin droid/Legends|assassin droids]], and [[Droideka/Legends|droidekas]]. He runs into [[Dexter Jettster/Legends|Dexter Jettster]], who tells him about a Jedi with a red lightsaber who ran into the nightclub. As Anakin pursues, Dexter tells him to remind Obi-Wan to come visit him in [[CoCo Town/Legends|CoCo Town]] more often.

Anakin enters the [[Bentho's Nightclub]], and [[Duel in Bentho's Nightclub|duels Trenox briefly]] inside, before Trenox flees yet again and Anakin chases him through a cargo bay. He finds [[Vandalor]], a bald male [[Chiss/Legends|Chiss]] and asks him where Trenox is. Vandalor says that Anakin is too late and that Trenox has probably already destroyed most of the [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]] in the two minutes since Anakin last saw him. The Chiss then leaves, claiming to have work to do. Anakin takes an [[Airspeeder/Legends|airspeeder]] to the Jedi Temple, but instead he finds [[Count/Legends|Count]] [[Dooku/Legends|Dooku]] waiting for him. Dooku comments on Anakin's cybernetic arm, saying it suits him, and after an exchange of comebacks Dooku excuses himself, as he has to destroy the [[Jedi Archives/Legends|Jedi Archives]]. Anakin protests at the prospect of the loss of a millennia {{C|sic}} of knowledge, but Dooku maintains that without the knowledge contained in the Archives, his Separatist allies will be able to win the war. Dooku then leaves Anakin with a C-B3 cortosis battle droid.

After dispatching it, Anakin contacts Obi-Wan with the newfound knowledge of the plot to destroy the Jedi Archives. Obi-Wan's transmission is cut out by a battle of some sort, and Anakin resolves to stop the assault on his own. After fighting through the Jedi Archives, he finds Trenox and they duel in a small room away from the main Archives. Anakin defeats him and receives a [[Hologram/Legends|hologram]] transmission from Master [[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]], who says reinforcements have arrived and that Anakin should meet with the Council. There, they inform him that Obi-Wan has been injured in a duel with Dooku and must recover from his wounds. They then tell Anakin to go to [[Metalorn/Legends|Metalorn]] and find and retrieve [[Wat Tambor/Legends|Wat Tambor]] and the plans to the [[Cortosis/Legends|cortosis]] droids.

===Metalorn===
{{Main|Mission to Metalorn (Clone Wars)}}
After landing on [[Metalorn/Legends|Metalorn]] in his [[Anakin Skywalker's red Delta-7 Aethersprite-class light interceptor|Jedi Starfighter]], Anakin infiltrates a [[droid factory]]. Inside, he contacts Master [[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]] and tells him that [[Dooku/Legends|Dooku]] indeed seems to be creating a new droid army. Yoda replies that Anakin must destroy the factory, as the Jedi Order cannot stand against such a threat. Deeper in the factory complex, Anakin meets the [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] brothers [[Vinoc]] and [[Karoc]] and engages them in [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|lightsaber combat]]. After defeating them, he finds some charges with which to destroy the factory's five reactors. However, [[Wat Tambor/Legends|Wat Tambor]] greets him before he can reach the fifth reactor. Tambor brings in the Chiss [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]], [[Vandalor]], to kill Anakin. Anakin defeats him and takes Wat Tambor prisoner before planting the last detonator.

As Anakin and Tambor escape from the destroyed factory, [[Doppelgänger of Dooku|Count Dooku]] arrives. The two duel, and Dooku says he senses confusion, anger, and pain in Anakin resulting from the death of [[Shmi Skywalker Lars/Legends|his mother]]. Dooku taunts Anakin, trying to make him embrace the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]] in battle. Anakin attacks Dooku until he falls down, says that [[Palpatine/Legends|his master]] foresaw his defeat and that Anakin has ensured the fall of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]], and fades away.

Back on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], Anakin informs the council that he has destroyed the factory, defeated Dooku, and apprehended Wat Tambor, whom [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] has requested is to be kept under the watch of his personal guard. [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]] and Yoda note that Anakin's skills are advancing rapidly, although Yoda notes that he senses a shadow approaching, and that the Republic is growing weary of war. Anakin gives his word that he will always be there for the Republic.

==Gameplay==
''Star Wars: The New Droid Army'' is a [[Wikipedia:Single-player video game|single-player]] [[Wikipedia:Action game|action game]].

==Development==
''Star Wars: The New Droid Army'' was developed by [[Helixe]] and published by [[THQ]] on [[November 14]], [[2002]], exclusively for the [[Wikipedia:Game Boy Advance|Game Boy Advance]]. The game's story was written by Helixe's [[Daniel Thron]] and Lead Designer [[David Konieczny|Dave Konieczny]], while the game design was led by artist [[Christopher Bruser]]. It was given an "E" rating by the [[Wikipedia:Entertainment Software Rating Board|ESRB]], with a content descriptor stating "violence."

==Continuity==
{{Quote|For years I've wanted to see some author use that neat dark side doppleganger trick Luke used in the ''Dark Empire'' graphic novel, where Leia and Han think they've rescued him from the dark side world Byss, and then once aboard the ''Millennium Falcon'', the doppleganger-Luke sort of mocks his rescuers, saying he's actually still on the planet.|Abel G. Peña's basis for the "Dooku" retcon|<ref name="SWFA">{{WebCite|url=https://www.myuselessknowledge.com/swfa/Ret-Con.html|text=SWFA: The Anatomy of a Ret-Con|author=Abel G. Peña|date=2005|work=[https://www.myuselessknowledge.com My Useless Knowledge]|archivedate=20190527071621}}</ref>}}
The reference book ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' originally dated the events of ''Star Wars: The New Droid Army'' to [[21 BBY/Legends|21 BBY]], ten [[Standard month/Legends|months]] after the events of [[Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones|''Star Wars'': Episode II ''Attack of the Clones'']]; however, this dating is no longer valid with the continuity changes introduced by the ''[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars]]'' television series.

[[File:Dookuclone.png|thumb|right|225px|Anakin Skywalker defeats "Dooku."]]
At the end of the game, Anakin defeats and apparently kills Count Dooku. However, this ending led to a continuity issue when Dooku appeared in later content. In response, ''[[Star Wars]]'' writer [[Abel G. Peña]] created a [[Retroactive continuity|retcon]] in ''[[The Official Star Wars Fact File]]'' [[The Official Star Wars Fact File 116|116]], saying that "Count Dooku" in ''Star Wars: The New Droid Army'' was not the real Dooku, but a [[dopplegänger]] projection created through [[The Force/Legends|the Force]]. The concept had previously appeared in the [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] series ''[[Star Wars: Dark Empire]]'', which served as the basis for Peña's retcon. This retcon was later mentioned in the [[2004]] novel ''[[Yoda: Dark Rendezvous]]''.<ref name="SWFA" />
